title: Dendrotrophe wrayi
intro: Dendrotrophe wrayi, also known as the Wrayi tree, is a plant species in the family Icacinaceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Dendrotrophe wrayi include triterpenoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dendrotrophe wrayi include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
